FILE blueprints/wpq4-grok-detached-busy.md
# WPQ4 — grok detached-command state classifies BUSY (F29 + F16-misprobe evidence)

Status: **FROZEN r5 — BUILD AUTHORITY** (design gate r4 GATE-YES 0B/0S/0N,
zero-decision buildable YES, tmp/orch/wpq4-design-gate-r4.md; rounds r1-r4:
4B+1B+1B -> 0, memos tmp/orch/wpq4-design-gate-r{1..4}.md; reviewer complete
prototype 44 passed). BUILD SEQUENCING: WPQ3 (in build, shared watchdog file)
lands FIRST; this WP builds on the post-WPQ3 fork HEAD with builder step 0 =
freshness re-check of every pinned file:line (watchdog :262-308/:171-177/:247-260
and test :381-406 WILL shift; semantic anchors govern). Fold logs r1-r5 archived
in git history (this file's prior commits 96b646e..d88acb8).

## Problem (evidence-cited)

Grok CLI executes shell commands as detached tasks: the pane shows a
`Worked for <N>s. <k> command(s) still running.` flow row with an IDLE-looking
composer (`❯`) and footer BELOW it. Two symptoms, both observed 2026-07-17:

1. **Watchdog false alarms (F29)**: `get_status` (grok_cli.py:345-374) finds
   no processing spinner and no end-anchored completion match, so the composer
   idle match (line 364) returns IDLE; stalled_callback_watchdog counts 120s
   → spurious `[watchdog] idle without callback`. Confirmed by supervisor peek
   on tester 9f3bfbf5 mid-pytest; 5 prior suspected firings (WPU1 arc).
2. **Mid-task delivery**: drain-2026-07-17a item 1 — victim 5ddd17e5 mid
   `sleep 90` detached task; inbox delivery opened attempts and injected into
   the live pane (01-f16-attempts.txt, 01-f16-late-callback.txt). The victim's
   next turn started while its command still ran.

Root cause: the provider has NO vocabulary for the detached-execution state.
COMPLETION_PATTERN (`^\s*(?:Turn completed in [\d.]+s\.|Worked for [\d.]+s\.)\s*$`)
is end-anchored, so the still-running suffix correctly prevents COMPLETED — but
nothing asserts BUSY, and idle chrome wins.

## D1 — RUNNING vocabulary (provider-level, canonical seam)

Add to `providers/grok_cli.py`:

```
RUNNING_PATTERN (pinned from observed row `Worked for 0.0s. 1 command still running.`):
^\s*Worked for [\d.]+s\.\s+\d+ commands? still running\.\s*$
```

Observed adjacent vocabulary (recon): a `Task started` row precedes the run;
it is NOT load-bearing (scrolls off) and gets no pattern.

- `get_status`: compute `last_running = self._last_match(RUNNING_PATTERN, clean_output)`.
  A running match newer than the newest completion match ⇒ `PROCESSING`,
  checked at the same tier as `last_processing` (before COMPLETED/IDLE arms).
  Ordering law identical to the existing processing-vs-completion comparison.
- `classify_screen`: rows matching RUNNING_PATTERN emit
  `ScreenSignal("progress", "RUNNING_PATTERN", index, row, <policy>)` where
  `<policy>` is pinned in D2.

## D2 — stale-quote policy: `exempt` (R1 answered NO by recon)

Recon fact R1: the still-running row is FROZEN — `Worked for 0.0s.` never ticks
during the detached run (ticking observed only on foreground Sleep/
Waiting-for-response states). Temporal corroboration therefore CANNOT
discriminate a real running row from a quoted one; the `corroborable` branch is
DELETED. Pinned policy:

- `classify_screen`: RUNNING rows emit
  `ScreenSignal("progress", "RUNNING_PATTERN", index, row, "exempt")` — same
  policy class as the existing `Waiting for response` exemption
  (grok_cli.py:389-391), so no new policy vocabulary is introduced.
- KNOWN RESIDUAL (accepted, named precisely per gate r1 S1): this WP INTRODUCES
  a new lexical false-busy surface — a quoted/echoed still-running row that is
  NEWER than a real completion row makes both `get_status` and viewport
  classification PROCESSING (empirically demonstrated at gate r1). It joins the
  same policy class as the pre-existing quoted `Waiting for response` exemption
  hole, is named in the ledger row as WP-introduced, and inherits any future
  exempt-row fix. No mitigation in this WP.

## D3 — watchdog interaction (decisive-signal suppression, two-phase, pinned)

Recon fact R3: the idle clock arms on IDLE/COMPLETED, grace 120s. Layer 1: with
D1, a live still-running pane classifies PROCESSING via `record_status`
(stalled_callback_watchdog.py:171-177) — the clock never arms in the common
case. Layer 2 pre-emit guard, pinned exactly:

- **Suppression condition (gate r1 B1)**: suppress iff the FRESH frame's
  provider classification is PROCESSING **and its deciding signal is
  RUNNING_PATTERN** (newest-flow ordering preserved). An any-match test is
  FORBIDDEN: real completed frames t10-t12 still contain a historical
  still-running row above the newer bare completion and MUST emit normally
  (positive control in D5).
- **Two-phase algorithm (gate r1 B2)** around the existing
  `collect_due_notifications` (:262-298) / `notify_due` (:300-308) seam:
  1. Under `_lock`: identify the due candidate; snapshot episode identity, the
     durable-callback check result, AND an eligibility token — at minimum
     `(idle_since, last_screen_fp)` (gate r2 B1: episodes mutate IN PLACE via
     `record_status` :171-177 and fingerprint liveness :247-260, so identity
     alone cannot detect un-arming during capture); do NOT set `fired`.
  2. Outside the lock: ONE fresh viewport-only capture (D-L1 contract,
     `_screens` never mutated) + classification. No backend I/O ever runs under
     the episode lock.
  3. Reacquire `_lock`: revalidate the SAME episode is live, unfired, still
     callback-free (same query order as step 1), AND still eligible: the
     eligibility token is byte-unchanged, `idle_since is not None`, and the
     grace window is still due. ANY eligibility change => drop, no emission, no
     re-arm side effect. Else if suppression condition holds: re-arm the grace
     clock, no emission. Otherwise: atomically set `fired = True` and hand the
     notice to `notify_due`.
- **Capture failure (pinned)**: guard is a positive-evidence suppressor only —
  capture-fail or classification-fail proceeds to emission exactly as the
  pre-guard watchdog would (a possible false alarm beats a silent stall).

Delivery: PROCESSING / exempt-progress is not deliverable => mid-task injection
(symptom 2, drain-17a item 1) closes with no delivery-engine change.

## D4 — semantics & edges

- A worker that deliberately leaves a long/immortal background command running
  keeps its terminal BUSY ⇒ inbox holds. This is CORRECT (same as a long model
  turn); bounded by command lifetime. HOL risk noted, no cap in this WP.
- Completion rows (`Worked for Ns.` bare) remain COMPLETED-vocabulary; a
  running row followed later by a bare completion row settles by newest-match
  ordering (existing law).
- No schema, DB, API, or cross-provider change. codex/claude providers
  untouched.

## D5 — tests (derive from PIN, not implementation)

- Unit (test_grok_cli_unit.py): frame fixtures from recon captures (REAL pane
  bytes, not synthetic): (a) running row + idle composer ⇒ PROCESSING;
  (b) running row then bare completion ⇒ COMPLETED; (c) plural/singular forms;
  (d) quoted running row as last flow row under D2's surviving policy.
- Wave4 screen-classification (test_wave4_delivery_state.py): RUNNING signal
  emitted with pinned policy tag; existing :1059 case updated from
  incidental-nonmatch to explicit RUNNING expectation; the old three-capture
  case at :1054 updated; AND the signal-name completeness roster at :792-838
  gains RUNNING_PATTERN (gate r1 S2 — enumerated so the builder finds no hidden
  expected edit).
- Watchdog (gate r1 B3, discriminating by construction): pre-arm a DUE
  stale-IDLE episode; the guard's single fresh capture returns real frame t07
  => assert suppress + grace re-arm (kills m4 — D1 alone cannot pass this);
  control: same setup returning t10 (newer completion) => notice proceeds;
  capture-fail => notice proceeds; episode replaced during capture =>
  revalidation drops, no emission, new episode untouched; PLUS the gate r2
  race controls: (i) same-episode `record_status(PROCESSING)` during capture =>
  drop, no emission (the un-arming race); (ii) same-episode fingerprint/grace
  reset during capture => drop; (iii) callback records during capture =>
  drop/no-fire, callback thread joins without lock starvation (gate r2 S2 —
  independently kills removal of the callback re-check / second durable query).
- Enumerated existing-fixture adaptation (gate r2 S1): the guard's second
  durable-callback query changes
  `test_watchdog_refresh_rearm_pending_callback_prevents_second_fire`
  (test_stalled_callback_watchdog.py:381-406) from a `[None, PENDING]`/2-call
  sequence to `[None, None, PENDING]`/3 calls — update that fixture exactly;
  no other existing watchdog test changes.
- Mutants (reviewer-owned adversarial set): m1 delete RUNNING_PATTERN arm in
  get_status; m2 drop signal emission in classify_screen; m3 invert
  running-vs-completion ordering; m4 remove the watchdog pre-emit fresh capture (killed ONLY by the pre-armed
  stale-IDLE t07 fixture above); m5 change exempt->corroborable (caught by the
  frozen-row fixture); m4b (reviewer option) skip step-3 revalidation.
  Each must be killed by a named test.

## D7 — count laws (OBSERVED-RUN ONLY, per GOLDEN-TIPS directive)

No arithmetic pins. OBSERVED focused baselines at `9eb0352` (gate r1, real
runs; exact scopes):

```
uv run pytest test/providers/test_grok_cli_unit.py            # 34 collected
uv run pytest test/services/test_wave4_delivery_state.py      # 48 collected
uv run pytest test/services/test_stalled_callback_watchdog.py # 37 collected
uv run pytest <all three>                                     # 119 passed
```

Lawful post-build deltas: counts grow ONLY by this WP's enumerated added/edited
nodes (D5 fixtures a-d, roster edit, and the SEVEN distinct named watchdog
guard test nodes: t07 suppress/re-arm, t10 emit, capture-fail emit,
episode-replacement drop, PROCESSING-during-capture drop,
fingerprint/grace-reset-during-capture drop, callback-during-capture drop —
one test node each, no grouping/parametrization collapse); 0 failed;
exact observed counts reported, never derived. Full-suite law: baseline
6252c/122d/6116p/14s/0f (observed drain-2026-07-17b, `--extra otel`,
examples/fleet/panel/.venv quarantined) — post-build run must show 0 failed and
growth only by the same enumerated nodes. mypy no-extras baseline 125/26
(api/main 9); requirement Δ0.

## D8 — execution

Builder edits ONLY: providers/grok_cli.py, services/stalled_callback_watchdog.py,
the two provider/wave4 test files, watchdog test file. COMPILE LAW applies (py_compile +
per-file --collect-only after every edited file). Hard stop on any deviation
from D7 observed laws or any edit outside the enumerated set.

## OPEN — none (recon closed R1/R2/R3; see facts file)

R1 counter-tick: NO for the detached row (frozen `0.0s`) — D2 pinned exempt.
R2 row bytes: singular observed; pattern covers plural defensively. `Task
started` exists, not load-bearing.
R3 watchdog predicate: idle clock arms on IDLE/COMPLETED, grace 120s — D3
layer-2 guard pinned.

FILE blueprints/wpq5-delivery-confirm-replay.md
# WPQ5 — F27 inferred delivery confirmation + F2 compact-boundary digest

Status: FROZEN r5 (2026-07-17) — BUILD AUTHORITY. Design gate r5 GATE-YES
0B/0S/0N, zero-decision buildable YES (tmp/orch/wpq5-design-gate-r5.md;
gate history r1-r5 in tmp/orch/wpq5-design-gate-r*.md + git log of this
file — older fold logs archived at FREEZE). Fold log r4->r5: gate r4 1B/2S/0N
(tmp/orch/wpq5-design-gate-r4.md) — B1 batch attempts: challenge
eligibility restricted to single-message attempts (len(batch)==1);
multi-member attempts inject the existing wire UNCHANGED and record no
attempt_challenge (screen/transcript confirmation + retry behavior
unchanged) — D5 q control + mutant m16. S1: package skill mirror
src/cli_agent_orchestrator/skills/cao-worker-protocols/SKILL.md added to
D8 with scripts/sync_skills.py + --check pinned. S2: trace-render
assertion pinned to existing node test_message_trace_inbox_matrix.py::
test_messages_trace_cli_table_and_json (builder choice deleted). Fold
log r3->r4: gate r3 2B/3S/0N
(tmp/orch/wpq5-design-gate-r3.md) — B1 FALSIFIED durable bearer token
(list/trace surfaces leak it; cross-message relay probe confirmed false
confirmation + retry loss): D1 REDESIGNED per-attempt WIRE-ONLY challenge,
raw value never persisted, SHA-256 stored as an `attempt_challenge` trace
event (SUPERVISOR RULING: evidence rides the ONE authorized trace-event
table — no attempt-row column, attempts stay immutable); anchor = MATCHING
attempt, not earliest. B2 DISSOLVED by the same redesign (minting moved to
the common delivery engine; `create_inbox_message` and
`create_logical_inbox_message` both untouched; named logical-mailbox test
added). S1 worker instruction pinned to the complete token. S2 "migration
file" DELETED — table/model/indexes pinned in clients/database.py, creation
owned by `Base.metadata.create_all` in `init_db` (no migration runner
exists). S3 `cli/commands/messages.py` added to roster — default trace
renders event kind/time.
Baseline fork: `9eb0352` (BUILD sequenced after WPQ3 and WPQ4 land — shared
inbox/settlement seams; step-0 freshness re-check mandatory, pinned line
numbers WILL shift).
Recon: `tmp/orch/wpq5-recon-facts.md` (payload-excluding-checksum-line hash at
its end). Incident anchor: msg 2819 → 3b1333a0, 2026-07-17 (GOLDEN-TIPS F27/F2).

## Problem (evidence-cited)

1. **F27 false-negative delivery_failed**: 3× ambiguous/confirmation_timeout
   attempts (ba4a/de6f/97cb) hit attempt_cap(3) →
   `transition_pending_to_delivery_failed` (inbox_service.py:1154-57;
   per-attempt settle :1481-88) while the receiver's pane showed receipt AND
   the receiver sent explicit ACKs (mids 2820/2822 + notice 2821, timestamps
   order-proven in recon §3). Consequence: spurious P9-tagged redelivery of a
   message the receiver already acted on.
2. **F2 compact-eaten callbacks**: a Claude compact boundary EATS
   already-delivered inbox messages from context (`status=delivered`, TUI
   content gone). Today there is NO replay/digest path (recon §5: `cao messages
   list/trace/ack` are read-only; no compact→replay detector §6) — the
   supervisor must remember to sweep manually.

## D1 — F27: wire-bound challenge confirmation (durable bearer token DELETED)

Gate r1 B4 refuted party/time correlation; gate r3 B1 refuted the durable
bearer token (any exact quote proves possession of a transferable string —
list/inbox surfaces return full PENDING bodies, so the token leaves the row
without the wire ever landing). Confirmation now requires proof bound to the
DELIVERY CHANNEL of a specific attempt:

- **Challenge (WIRE-ONLY; SINGLE-MESSAGE ATTEMPTS ONLY — gate r4 B1)**:
  eligible IFF the attempt's batch has exactly ONE member message
  (`deliver_pending` groups contiguous same-sender/type rows into one
  attempt, inbox_service.py:949-959; settlement is batch-atomic,
  clients/database.py:3411-3425 — a last-suffix token cannot lawfully
  prove the other members). Multi-member attempts: inject the existing
  combined wire UNCHANGED, record NO `attempt_challenge`; screen/
  transcript confirmation and retry behavior stay byte-identical to
  today. For an eligible attempt: `token128` = 32 lowercase hex chars
  from `secrets.token_hex(16)`. Inside `deliver_pending`, under the terminal
  delivery lock, immediately before provider injection: splice
  ` | mid <message_id>:<token128>` into a COPY of the wire bytes by replacing
  ONLY the LAST exact authentic wrapper suffix for `sender_id` (`rfind`;
  quoted earlier wrappers untouched). If the authentic suffix is absent, the
  wire is injected unchanged and NO challenge is recorded for that attempt
  (confirmation is opportunistic). The durable inbox row is byte-preserved in
  ALL cases — raw challenges exist nowhere but the injected wire.
- **Evidence (SUPERVISOR RULING)**: in the SAME transaction that creates the
  attempt row, append trace event `kind="attempt_challenge"`, payload
  `{"attempt_uuid", "challenge_sha256"}` (SHA-256 hex of the raw token128).
  No attempt-row column; settled attempts stay immutable; list/trace surfaces
  expose only the hash, which is not a bearer.
- **Confirmation predicate**: an inbox row with `sender_id == attempted
  receiver`, body containing a delimiter-aware `mid <id>:<hex32>` token
  (bounded by non-hex on both ends — `mid 12` never matches `mid 129`) whose
  `<id>` equals the message id AND whose SHA-256 equals a recorded
  `attempt_challenge` hash in that message's lineage, and normalized-UTC
  `created_at` strictly after the MATCHING attempt's `started_at` (anchor =
  the attempt whose hash matched, never earliest/latest). Cross-message
  relay is structurally rejected: a hash matching a different message's
  attempt confirms nothing for this message.
- **Clock law (gate r1 B1 + gate r2 S3)**: `inbox.created_at` is local-naive;
  resolver PINNED as `tzlocal.get_localzone()` (make `tzlocal` a direct
  dependency if the resolved graph does not already expose it); normalize
  `created_at.replace(tzinfo=get_localzone()).astimezone(timezone.utc)` with
  `fold=0` on DST-ambiguous values; attempt values are UTC-naive and are
  compared via `replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)`. The incident-2819 fixture MUST
  assert raw comparison misses and normalized comparison hits.
- **Seam A — per-attempt** (O2 ruling adopted verbatim): inside
  `deliver_pending` under the terminal delivery lock (inbox_service.py:851-860
  at 9eb0352), on a lawful challenge hit: atomically settle the still-OPEN
  attempt and the DELIVERING parent delivered, append the
  `inferred_delivered` event, run the existing confirmed callback/watchdog
  effects, evict defer state, return before ambiguity wake/retry.
- **Seam B — cap** (gate r1 B2): settled attempts are immutable
  (`settle_delivery_attempt` returns False; clients/database.py:3344-3357).
  A NEW atomic helper CASes the parent `PENDING -> DELIVERED` (same CAS
  discipline as PENDING->DELIVERY_FAILED at :4274-4286) and appends the
  `kind="inferred_delivered"` event (payload `{"reply_message_id",
  "challenge_sha256", "anchor_attempt_uuid", "normalized_reply_at"}`) in the
  SAME transaction — no settled attempt row mutated or resurrected. The cap
  path consults the predicate BEFORE the DELIVERY_FAILED CAS.
- **Coverage**: because minting lives in the delivery engine, raw
  (`create_inbox_message`) and logical (`mailbox_service.
  create_logical_inbox_message`, api/main.py:2990-3000 route) rows are BOTH
  covered with zero insert-path edits; a named logical-row test is required
  (D5 p).
- **No challenge quoted => behavior byte-identical to today** (fails to
  delivery_failed; redelivery unchanged). Retryability is never removed on
  weak evidence — the weak tier does not exist.
- **Worker-protocol ask (docs, enumerated; gate r3 S1)**:
  `skills/cao-worker-protocols` gains one line: ACK/callback messages quote
  the COMPLETE received `mid <id>:<hex32>` token VERBATIM (bare `mid <id>` is
  rejected by the predicate). Optional-compliance: confirmation is
  opportunistic.

## D2 — F2: compact digest with time-bucketed fence (exact-once DELETED)

Gate r1 B5 proved per-boundary identity unrepresentable (retries mint new
binding ids; real compacts reuse every identity field). PINNED redesign:

- **Seam (O1 adopted)**: new
  `mailbox_service.publish_compact_boundary_digest(terminal_id, *, window_min,
  now_utc)` invoked from `api/main.py::bind_transcript` (:2103-2136)
  immediately after successful `create_transcript_binding` when
  `source == "compact"`.
- **Fence (time-bucketed, honest about transport limits)**: suppress if a
  compact-digest not…17840 tokens truncated…(mid 2461: first attempt untagged NULL lineage, redelivery 3011c398 visible tag + redelivery_tag evidence + prior_attempt_uuid). PLUS 3 organic F13 firings on supervisor notices to the busy tester (mids 2460/2463/2466, confirmation_timeout ×3 each → failed + caller notice, queue moving). Side find: grok "Waiting for response…" spinner classified IDLE → attempts open mid-turn → cap burn on meta-notices — F16 candidate |
| Redeploy verify-race fix (D1–D5) | after restart, redeploy polls ≤30s for the listener then prints phase-labeled truth (`server: current (...)`, exit 0 on good redeploy); timeout → hinted not-running + exit 1; `deployment_status()` stays pure; no-restart path byte-identical | inner `a4cf873` | live: next user-run `cao redeploy --yes` after activation → phase lines + `server: current`, exit 0 (the exact false-negative the user hit must not recur) | drained-pass | 2026-07-15 ~09:50 local, user-run activation restart: output showed `restarting cao-server...` + `waiting for server to come back up... (0s)/(1s)` + `CLI path: current (0 files differ)` + `server: current (restarted and listening on :9889)` — the exact false `server: not-running` from 08:01 did NOT recur; phase lines exist only in the fixed code |
| Wave 2 P6+P8min+P4 (D1–D11) | P6: launch resolves absolute artifact root once, injects reserved `CAO_ARTIFACTS_DIR` (override must be absolute, window overlay cannot replace it), 5 active profiles fail-closed with mandatory violation line; P8-min: manifest sections honest `ok/error/not_collected` (tools+ledger not_collected), `complete` = all declared ok, supervisors role-derived id list, legacy `supervisor_terminal_id` widens string|null; P4: `cao base register` → POST /bases/register writes GLOBAL NULL-source rows, strict registration validation vs byte-identical assign compatibility, 12-code HTTP 400 domain envelope (incl. non-UTF-8 rollout → artifact_identity_mismatch) | inner `33aad1c` | live post-activation: (1) new launch → `$CAO_ARTIFACTS_DIR` absolute in worker env, worker writes land there; unset-var probe → verbatim violation line + refusal; (2) `cao session manifest` full AND compact brief both show not_collected sections, complete:false reasons visible; (3) register base from dead-session artifacts → row visible, mutate cwd → fork gets stale preamble; corrupt/wrong inputs → 400 stable codes, never 500 | drained-pass (2026-07-15 grok_tester a08d4eb4 r1: L1a/L2/L3a/L3c PASS; L1b found install.sh never enumerated grok_doc_keeper.md — line added, profile installed, verified byte-identical) | blueprint FROZEN r6 (16-direction brainstorm → user pick “Slices + two binders”); diff gate r1 1B/1S/1N → r2 0/0/0 SHIP YES; suite 5261 green, focused 188, mypy delta 0 (133/133 fresh no-cache both sides); outer profile rewrites ride same activation train | 
| Wave 3B supervisor mailbox (D1–D10) | logical mailbox (session_name,role) survives supervisor churn: claim-based publication with CAS + incarnation history, late-bound logical delivery pastes successor-only under forced-generation race (interrupted→confirmed lineage), startup adoption tags historical rows, replay list/ack/digest surfaces scope-gated, both session-create CLIs project typed 409/409/500 | inner `9058a797` | live post-activation: (1) relaunch supervisor same session → digest exactly-once, PENDING rows follow to new incarnation; (2) send to mb_* then force generation bump mid-delivery → paste lands successor-only; (3) `cao messages list --to me` pagination + ack fences (not_current_incarnation, ack_out_of_range); (4) DELETE mailbox → mailbox_in_use/mailbox_busy codes, straggler settles delivery_failed/mailbox_deleted | drained-pass (P1 re-probe next churn) | blueprint FROZEN r8 (design grind 6B→8B→3B→2B→2B→1B→1B→0, grok clean since r5); build aaff47a3 → fix 18a94e3b → tests-only 9058a797; diff gate r1 2B/1S → r2 1B → r3 0/0/0 SHIP YES; suite 5300/13/101, acceptance 37 discriminating-under-mutation, fresh mypy 121→118 (Δ−3); rides next activation train. ## Activation facts (current, load-bearing)
- **USER RULING 2026-07-17: the activation train runs immediately after WPU1
  ships** (diff gate SHIP-YES + ledger row): user runs `./install.sh` +
  `systemctl --user restart cao-server` (prefer `cao redeploy --yes`, E4
  residual) — this KILLS the session; then the NEW session drains everything
  `pending activation`: WPQ1 + WPQ2 checks, wave4-livefix F16 re-drain +
  probe-17, Wave 3B P1 re-probe, F17 keeper-install check, G7a/G7b probes,
  WPU1's F21-dialog + orphan-kill drains. WPQ3 BUILD starts only after that
  (on activated code, rebased onto the WPU1 merge per its freeze clause).
- **Activation**: cao-server runs a uv tool COPY (`~/.local/share/uv/tools/…`), NOT
  editable. Working-tree edits go live ONLY via `./install.sh` THEN
  `systemctl --user restart cao-server` — user runs it, **ask first** (kills every live
  session incl. this one). CLI-path features activate on install.sh alone (uv copy);
  MCP-server-path features need the restart. Verify with `cao verify deploy` before
  trusting a live drain. Prefer `cao redeploy` (confirm-gated) at the next activation
  (E4 residual: still never exercised — use it for the fix-batch activation).
- Terminal IDs churn at every restart; all warm workers die — re-run `echo $CAO_TERMINAL_ID`.
  Only base registrations survive; their SOURCE terminals do NOT (F10; fix in this train).
- providers.toml is re-read per terminal launch (model changes need no restart); agent
  profiles go live via `cao install` at next spawn. `default_fork_base` is SET for
  codex + grok_cli (bare assign auto-forks; `fork_from="cold"` opts out).
- Codex "quota exceeded" banner is FALSE on the 中转站 relay, REAL on ChatGPT
  subscription — ask which backend before pausing the codex lane (memory
  codex-relay-quota-banner-false).
